name: Command Pattern with Undo/Redo Stack
description: Implement the command pattern with undo/redo support across two modules. Module command_types.py must define: Command abstract base class with abstract methods execute(state: dict) -> Any and undo(state: dict) -> None and a name property (str); CommandResult dataclass with fields command_name (str), value (Any), timestamp (float). Module command_invoker.py must import from command_types.py and implement Invoker class with methods: execute(command: Command) runs command.execute and pushes onto undo stack, clears redo stack, returns CommandResult; undo() pops undo stack, calls command.undo, pushes onto redo stack; redo() pops redo stack, calls command.execute, pushes back onto undo stack; can_undo() -> bool; can_redo() -> bool; history() -> list[str] (names of all currently undoable commands in order). Provide concrete commands: SetCommand(key, value) and DeleteCommand(key) operating on the state dict.
test_imports: from command_invoker import Invoker, SetCommand, DeleteCommand
signature:
# command_types.py
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any
import time

class Command(ABC):
    @property
    @abstractmethod
    def name(self) -> str: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def execute(self, state: dict) -> Any: ...
    @abstractmethod
    def undo(self, state: dict) -> None: ...

@dataclass
class CommandResult:
    command_name: str
    value: Any
    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=time.monotonic)

# command_invoker.py
from command_types import Command, CommandResult

class Invoker:
    def __init__(self, state: dict): ...
    def execute(self, command: Command) -> CommandResult: ...
    def undo(self) -> bool: ...
    def redo(self) -> bool: ...
    def can_undo(self) -> bool: ...
    def can_redo(self) -> bool: ...
    def history(self) -> list[str]: ...

class SetCommand(Command): ...
class DeleteCommand(Command): ...
tests:
def test_can_undo_redo_initial():
    inv = Invoker({})
    assert inv.can_undo() is False
    assert inv.can_redo() is False

def test_new_command_clears_redo():
    state = {}
    inv = Invoker(state)
    inv.execute(SetCommand('a', 1))
    inv.undo()
    assert inv.can_redo() is True
    inv.execute(SetCommand('b', 2))
    assert inv.can_redo() is False

def test_history_names():
    inv = Invoker({})
    inv.execute(SetCommand('a', 1))
    inv.execute(SetCommand('b', 2))
    inv.execute(DeleteCommand('a'))
    h = inv.history()
    assert len(h) == 3
    assert 'set' in h[0].lower() or 'set' in h[1].lower()

def test_set_overwrite_undo():
    state = {'x': 'old'}
    inv = Invoker(state)
    inv.execute(SetCommand('x', 'new'))
    assert state['x'] == 'new'
    inv.undo()
    assert state['x'] == 'old'

def test_command_result():
    inv = Invoker({})
    r = inv.execute(SetCommand('x', 99))
    assert isinstance(r, CommandResult)
    assert r.value == 99 or r.command_name
constraints: Each command must record enough info in execute to undo correctly
Executing a new command clears the redo stack
undo/redo return False when their stack is empty (do not raise)
history() reflects the undo stack only (not redo)
SetCommand on existing key must restore the previous value on undo, not delete
DeleteCommand on missing key is a no-op but still pushes onto undo stack
